R-M269.origin: the Pontic-Caspian steppe and early Bronze Age Europe
R-M269.story: R-M269 is the most common paternal lineage in Western Europe. It expanded rapidly during the Bronze Age, carried by the steppe-ancestry migrations that reshaped European populations roughly 4,000–5,000 years ago.

R-P312.story: R-P312 is a major Western European branch of R-M269, strongly associated with the Bell Beaker culture. It is especially frequent today in Iberia, France, Britain and Ireland.

R-U106.story: R-U106 is the principal Germanic branch of R-M269, common across the Low Countries, Germany, Scandinavia and the British Isles. It spread with the same steppe-derived populations that carried R-P312 westward.

R-M173.origin: the Eurasian steppe after the last Ice Age
R-M173.story: R-M173 (R1b) is one of the great paternal lineages of Western Eurasia. Its descendant branches came to dominate Western Europe in the Bronze Age.

R-M198.origin: Eastern Europe and the Eurasian steppe
R-M198.story: R-M198 (R1a) is widespread from Scandinavia and Eastern Europe through Central and South Asia. It expanded with steppe pastoralists and is closely tied to the spread of Indo-European languages.

I-M170.story: I-M170 is among the oldest paternal lineages native to Europe, present among the continent's hunter-gatherers before the arrival of farming and steppe ancestry.

I-M253.story: I-M253 (I1) is a characteristically Northern European lineage, common in Scandinavia and areas touched by later Germanic and Norse expansions.

I-M223.story: I-M223 is a branch of I2 found across Northwestern and Central Europe, with deep roots among European hunter-gatherer populations.

E-M35.story: E-M35 arose in Northeast Africa and its branches spread into the Mediterranean, the Near East and Europe over many millennia. The European branch E-V13 is associated with Neolithic and later expansions in the Balkans.

J-M267.story: J-M267 (J1) is a Near Eastern lineage common across the Arabian Peninsula, the Levant and the Caucasus, with links to both early farmers and later pastoralist populations.

J-M172.story: J-M172 (J2) is tied to the early farming populations of the Fertile Crescent and spread into the Mediterranean, Europe and South Asia with the expansion of agriculture.

G-M201.story: G-M201 is strongly associated with the first farmers of Anatolia and the Near East who brought agriculture into Europe during the Neolithic.

N-M231.origin: East Asia, later spreading across northern Eurasia
N-M231.story: N-M231 spread across northern Eurasia and is common today among Uralic-speaking and northern European and Siberian populations, especially in Finland and the Baltic.

Q-M242.story: Q-M242 is the principal paternal lineage of Indigenous peoples of the Americas, carried across Beringia, and is also found across Central Asia and Siberia.

T-M184.story: T-M184 is an uncommon lineage with Near Eastern origins, scattered today around the Mediterranean, East Africa and South Asia.

H.origin: the Near East, then much of Europe
H.story: Haplogroup H is the most common maternal lineage in Europe, carried by roughly half of people of European descent. It expanded across the continent after the last Ice Age.

HV.story: HV is the ancestral lineage to haplogroup H, with deep roots in the Near East before its descendants spread into Europe.

V.story: Haplogroup V re-expanded from a refuge in Southwestern Europe after the Ice Age and reaches its highest frequencies among the Saami and in Iberia.

J.story: Haplogroup J spread into Europe with early farmers from the Near East and is found across Europe, the Near East and North Africa.

T.story: Haplogroup T is associated with the spread of agriculture from the Near East into Europe and is common around the Mediterranean.

K.story: Haplogroup K, a branch of U8, is common in Europe and the Near East and was carried by some of Europe's earliest farming communities.

U.story: Haplogroup U is one of the oldest maternal lineages in Western Eurasia, present among European hunter-gatherers tens of thousands of years ago.

U5.story: Haplogroup U5 is the characteristic maternal lineage of Europe's ancient hunter-gatherers, found in the continent long before farming arrived.

X.origin: the Near East, with a wide later distribution
X.story: Haplogroup X has an unusually broad distribution, found in Europe, the Near East, North Africa and among some Indigenous peoples of North America.

I.story: Haplogroup I is a relatively uncommon maternal lineage spread across Europe and the Near East.

W.origin: the Near East and South Asia
W.story: Haplogroup W is found from Europe through the Near East to South Asia, reflecting ancient west-Eurasian dispersals.

N.story: Haplogroup N is one of the two great macro-lineages that arose as modern humans expanded out of Africa, ancestral to most non-African maternal lineages.

R.story: Haplogroup R is a major early branch within macro-haplogroup N and the ancestor of many of the most common maternal lineages across Eurasia.

L3.story: Haplogroup L3 arose in East Africa and is the lineage from which all non-African maternal haplogroups descend, carried by the populations that left Africa.

WGS.what: Reads your whole genome, so it covers every part of your DNA — paternal line, maternal line and ancestry.

WGS_LOW_PASS.what: Reads your whole genome at low depth — broad coverage of every region, with less certainty at any single position.
WGS_LOW_PASS.limits: Shallow depth means some fine details are less certain.

WGS_HIFI.what: Reads your whole genome with long, highly accurate reads — excellent for resolving your paternal line and hard-to-read regions.

WGS_NANOPORE.what: Reads your whole genome with long reads — good genome-wide coverage including difficult regions.

WGS_CLR.what: Reads your whole genome with long reads — broad coverage across every region.

WES.what: Reads the protein-coding parts of your genome (the exome).
WES.limits: Targets coding regions, so it is not used for lineage or ancestry results here.

BIG_Y_500.what: Reads your Y chromosome in depth to place your paternal line precisely.
BIG_Y_500.limits: Covers only the Y chromosome — no maternal line or ancestry composition.

BIG_Y_700.what: Reads your Y chromosome in depth to place your paternal line very precisely, including rare recent branches.
BIG_Y_700.limits: Covers only the Y chromosome — no maternal line or ancestry composition.

Y_ELITE.what: Sequences your Y chromosome to place your paternal line in detail.
Y_ELITE.limits: Covers only the Y chromosome — no maternal line or ancestry composition.

Y_PRIME.what: Sequences your Y chromosome to place your paternal line.
Y_PRIME.limits: Covers only the Y chromosome — no maternal line or ancestry composition.

TARGETED_Y.what: Targets your Y chromosome to determine your paternal line.
TARGETED_Y.limits: Covers only the Y chromosome — no maternal line or ancestry composition.

TARGETED_MT.what: Targets your mitochondrial DNA to determine your maternal line.
TARGETED_MT.limits: Covers only mitochondrial DNA — no paternal line or ancestry composition.

MT_FULL_SEQUENCE.what: Reads your complete mitochondrial genome to place your maternal line precisely.
MT_FULL_SEQUENCE.limits: Covers only mitochondrial DNA — no paternal line or ancestry composition.

MT_PLUS.what: Reads key regions of your mitochondrial DNA to estimate your maternal line.
MT_PLUS.limits: Covers only mitochondrial DNA, and at lower resolution than a full sequence.

MT_CR_ONLY.what: Reads the control region of your mitochondrial DNA for a broad maternal-line estimate.
MT_CR_ONLY.limits: Low-resolution maternal line only — no paternal line or ancestry.

YDNA_SNP_PACK_FTDNA.what: Tests a targeted set of Y-chromosome markers to refine your paternal line.
YDNA_SNP_PACK_FTDNA.limits: Covers only selected Y markers — no maternal line or ancestry.

YDNA_PANEL_YSEQ.what: Tests a panel of Y-chromosome markers to refine your paternal line.
YDNA_PANEL_YSEQ.limits: Covers only selected Y markers — no maternal line or ancestry.

YDNA_SNP_PANEL.what: Tests a panel of Y-chromosome markers to place your paternal line.
YDNA_SNP_PANEL.limits: Covers only selected Y markers — no maternal line or ancestry.

ARRAY_BISDNA.what: Genotypes a set of Y-chromosome markers to place your paternal line.
ARRAY_BISDNA.limits: Covers selected Y markers — no maternal line or ancestry composition.

ARRAY_23ANDME_V5.what: Genotypes hundreds of thousands of markers across your genome — enough for ancestry composition and a broad read of your paternal and maternal lines.
ARRAY_23ANDME_V5.limits: Reads selected positions, not your whole genome, so lineage detail is limited compared with sequencing.

ARRAY_23ANDME_V4.what: Genotypes markers across your genome for ancestry composition and a broad read of your lineages.
ARRAY_23ANDME_V4.limits: Reads selected positions, not your whole genome.

ARRAY_ANCESTRY_V2.what: Genotypes markers across your genome for ancestry composition and a broad read of your lineages.
ARRAY_ANCESTRY_V2.limits: Reads selected positions, not your whole genome.

ARRAY_FTDNA_FF.what: Genotypes markers across your genome for ancestry and relative matching.
ARRAY_FTDNA_FF.limits: Reads selected positions, not your whole genome.

ARRAY_MYHERITAGE.what: Genotypes markers across your genome for ancestry composition and relative matching.
ARRAY_MYHERITAGE.limits: Reads selected positions, not your whole genome.

ARRAY_LIVINGDNA.what: Genotypes markers across your genome for ancestry composition and a broad read of your lineages.
ARRAY_LIVINGDNA.limits: Reads selected positions, not your whole genome.

European.blurb: European ancestry spans the continent from Iberia and the British Isles to Scandinavia, the Balkans and the East European plain — shaped by ancient hunter-gatherers, Near Eastern farmers and Bronze Age steppe migrations.

African.blurb: African ancestry is the most genetically diverse on Earth, reflecting the deep history of human populations across West, East, Central and Southern Africa.

East Asian.blurb: East Asian ancestry centers on China, Japan, Korea and mainland Southeast Asia, with deep roots in the populations of the region.

South Asian.blurb: South Asian ancestry covers the Indian subcontinent — India, Pakistan, Bangladesh and Sri Lanka — blending ancient indigenous, Iranian-farmer and steppe components.

Admixed American.blurb: Admixed American ancestry reflects the blending of Indigenous American, European and African populations across the Americas.

Middle Eastern.blurb: Middle Eastern ancestry is associated with the Levant, Arabia, Anatolia and the wider Near East — a crossroads of early farming and later population movements.

Central Asian & Siberian.blurb: Central Asian and Siberian ancestry spans the vast Eurasian interior, from the steppe to the northern forests, with a long history of pastoralist and hunter movements.

Oceanian.blurb: Oceanian ancestry is associated with the Indigenous populations of New Guinea, Melanesia and the wider Pacific.
